claim_shape: (Currently Amended) A method of dynamic channel allocation for an internet of things (IoT) device, the method comprising: monitoring activity of one or more devices wirelessly communicating with a node; determining, based on the monitored activity, an amount of unallocated spectrum in a communications channel used by the node to wirelessly communicate with the one or more devices; determining for a first device of the one or more devices, a bandwidth requirement associated with a first communication protocol used by the first device; determining that the amount of unallocated spectrum is less than the bandwidth requirement associated with the first communication protocol; comparing the amount of unallocated spectrum to the bandwidth requirement associated with the communication protocol; and based on the comparisondetermination, dynamically allocating at least a portion of the unallocated spectrum to a second device of the one or more devices, wherein the second device uses a second communication protocol that is different from the first communication protocol used by the first device. Currently amended 2
remedy: Examiner's ground: Claims 1, 3, 4 and 6-17 are rejected under 35 U.S.C. 103 as being unpatentable over Bye et al. (US 2022/0132320), hereinafter Bye in view of Gupta et al. (US 2018/0270820), hereinafter Gupta further in view of Pikkarainen et al. (US 2011/0255425), hereinafter Pikkarainen.
